---
title: "Georreferencing"
author: "ACMG"
format: html
editor: visual
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}

packages <- c("tidyverse", "readxl", "readr", "data.table", "dplyr", "stringr", "ggplot2", "sf", "fs", "rnaturalearth", "rnaturalearthdata", "rio", "countrycode","maps", "stringi","rnaturalearthdata","devtools", "pack", "cli", "remotes", "countrycode","progress", "tidygeocoder", "dplyr", "readr", "purrr","writexl","tidygeocoder","readr")

# Install packages not yet installed [for the first time]
installed_packages <- packages %in% rownames(installed.packages())
if (any(installed_packages == FALSE)) {
  install.packages(packages[!installed_packages])
}

# Packages loading
invisible(lapply(packages, require, character.only = TRUE)) # remove "invisible" to see whether the packages are loaded/available. 

Sys.setlocale("LC_ALL", "Spanish_Spain.1252") # en Windows


```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
dfCR <- read.csv2("C:/Desktop/Voluntariado epifitas/EpIG_volunteership/data/Georreferencing/1.Raw/georef__CR2024.csv",sep = ",")

names(dfCR) # check names of the columns
View(dfCR) # if you want to open and see the file
str(dfCR) #solo
head(dfCR) # if you want to see more or less the structure of the dataframe (df)
```

#Crear columna de cambios y de aprobación

```{r}

if (!"ok.2" %in% names(dfCR)) dfCR$ok.2 <- dfCR$.ok_for_analysis #Verificación de la existencia del nombre ok.2, si no existe que se cree
if (!"discardReason" %in% names(dfCR)) dfCR$discardReason <- NA_character_
view(dfCR)

# flag AMAZ_47 for discard
idx <- which(dfCR_ref$occurrenceID) 
if (length(idx)) {
  dfCR$discardReason[idx] <- "Impossible to correct, too vague coordinate"
}



```

#Categorizar en simple, detallado e imposible de corregir

```{r}
dfCR <- dfCR %>%
mutate(
georef_class = case_when(
is.na(locality) | str_squish(locality) == "" ~ "non_georef",
str_detect(locality, regex(
"\\d+\\s*km|carretera|road|highway|reserva|parque nacional|río|quebrada|cerro|vereda",
ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "detailed",
TRUE ~ "simple"
)
)
```

#Filtrar


```{r}
dfCR <- dfCR %>%
  filter(.ok_for_analysis == FALSE,
         !is.na(decimalLatitude),
         !is.na(decimalLongitude)) %>%
  select(
    occurrenceID,
    decimalLatitude, decimalLongitude,
    locality, country, countryCode,
    changes
  )
unique(dfCR$country)
```

```{r}
dfCR1 <- dfCR %>%
  filter(country=="Costa Rica") %>%
  select(
    occurrenceID,
    decimalLatitude, decimalLongitude,
    locality, country, countryCode,
    changes,
    georef_class
  )
unique(dfCR1$country)

```

#Exportar a excel


```{r}
write.csv(dfCR1, file = "C:/Desktop/Voluntariado epifitas/entrenamiento/CR1.csv")
```

```{r}
write_xlsx(dfCR1, "C:/Desktop/Voluntariado epifitas/entrenamiento/CR_simple.xlsx")
```

```{r}
#coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
dfCR$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters <- NA

```

# Cargar coordenadas digitalizadas de csv

```{r}

dfCR_ref <- read.csv2("C:/Desktop/Voluntariado epifitas/entrenamiento/CR_simple.csv",sep = ";")
str(dfCR_ref)
```

#Codigo para unir data digitalizada y a data original

```{r}
str(dfCR)

dfCR_ref$decimalLongitude <- as.numeric(dfCR_ref$decimalLongitude) #Convertir a numerico
dfCR_ref$decimalLatitude <- as.numeric(dfCR_ref$decimalLatitude)
dfCR_ref$insertidumbre <- as.numeric(dfCR_ref$insertidumbre)
dfCR$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters <- as.numeric(dfCR$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)
dfCR$decimalLongitude <- as.numeric(dfCR$decimalLongitude)
dfCR$decimalLatitude <- as.numeric(dfCR$decimalLatitude)
dfCR <- dfCR %>% # Archivo original

  left_join( 
    dfCR_ref %>% select( # Archivo a unir
      occurrenceID, #Este es el campo para union de las columnas
      longitude_corregida = decimalLongitude, 
      latitude_corregida  = decimalLatitude,
      incertidumbre = insertidumbre,
      lo_corr = Localidad
    ),
    by = "occurrenceID" # Campo por medio del cual se realiza la unión
  ) %>%
  

  mutate(
    decimalLongitude = coalesce(longitude_corregida, decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude  = coalesce(latitude_corregida,  decimalLatitude),
    coordinateUncertaintyInMeters = coalesce(incertidumbre,  coordinateUncertaintyInMeters),
    locality = coalesce(lo_corr,  locality)
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Eliminamos las columnas temporales para dejar la base de datos limpia
  select(-longitude_corregida, -latitude_corregida, -lo_corr, -incertidumbre)
```

```{r}

codeCR <- dfCR_ref$occurrenceID
codeCR

# make sure ok.2 exists
if (!"ok.2" %in% names(dfCR)) dfCR$ok.2 <- dfCR$.ok_for_analysis #Verificación de la existencia del nombre ok.2, si no existe que se cree

# make sure discardReason exists
if (!"discardReason" %in% names(dfCR)) dfCR$discardReason <- NA_character_



for (code in codeCR) {
  idx <- which(dfCR_ref$occurrenceID == code)
  if (length(idx)) {
    dfCR$ok.2[idx] <- TRUE
    dfCR$discardReason[idx] <- "Corrected: points were corrected according to locality"
  }
}



```
